title: America's Birthday: The Declaration of Independence
description: This coloring page introduces young learners to the founding of America through a simplified illustration of the signing of the Declaration of Independence. It helps students visualize this important historical moment while developing fine motor skills.
instructions: Color the picture using crayons or colored pencils. Think about what colors might have been used in clothing during this time. Read the learning facts with a parent or teacher.

content.image: An easy-to-color illustration of the Continental Congress signing the Declaration of Independence. The scene shows several founding fathers gathered around a table with a large document. John Hancock is shown with a quill pen signing the document. An American flag with 13 stars is displayed in the background. The illustration has bold, clear lines appropriate for young children to color.

elements.name: Declaration of Independence
elements.description: The important document on the table

elements.name: First American Flag
elements.description: The flag with 13 stars in the background

learningFacts: The Declaration of Independence was signed in 1776.
John Hancock wrote his name very large on the document.
The Declaration of Independence helped America become its own country.
The first American flag had 13 stars for the 13 original colonies.
July 4th is celebrated as America's birthday.
